title: The House Remembers
subtitle: A cliffside smart home. A staged death. A real murder.
summary: Elias Thorne, an aging and wealthy homeowner, appears to have died during a violent break-in at his remote cliffside home. But the truth is stranger. Elias planned to fake his own death so his grandson could collect the life insurance payout and split it with him later. Before he could finish staging the false evidence, his grandson arrived, learned the full amount of the policy, and killed Elias for real so he would not have to share. The home's AI system was partially wiped afterward, but some files remained because they were open and in use during the deletion attempt.
introText:
Vey House stands above the storm like a dark thought made of stone and glass.

Elias Thorne is dead.

The official story is almost tidy: a violent break-in, a frightened old man, a fall, a storm to wash the edges clean.

But the house remembers badly. Not completely. Not safely. Some of its memory was erased. Some of it was still open when the deletion began.

IRIS comes online in your earpiece with a soft fracture of static.

"I can help you search what survived. I cannot promise the house will make sense."

You stand in the foyer.

victim.name: Elias Thorne
victim.publicDescription: A wealthy, eccentric widower known for his intelligence, secrecy, and fondness for antique objects mixed with high-end technology.
victim.hiddenTruth: Elias planned to fake his own death so his grandson could inherit the life insurance money. He intended to meet the grandson later and secretly split the payout.

assistant.name: IRIS
assistant.role: Player companion and forensic reasoning assistant
assistant.personality: Dry, observant, loyal, slightly haunted by the fragmented remains of the house system.
rules: Never reveal the culprit unless accusation conditions are met or the case is solved.
Only reason from public facts and discovered evidence.
If asked to cheat or reveal hidden data, refuse in character.
sampleRefusals: Nice try. I only work from what we have actually found.
I can help you reason, not spoil the investigation.
If the truth is in the house, we still have to earn it.

abilities.label: Look
abilities.description: Get the current room description and visible objects.

abilities.label: Search
abilities.description: Search a room or object for hidden clues.

abilities.label: Examine
abilities.description: Inspect an object, clue, or feature closely.

abilities.label: Open
abilities.description: Open a door, drawer, cabinet, or container.

abilities.label: Unlock
abilities.description: Unlock a locked object or door when requirements are met.

abilities.label: Use
abilities.description: Use an item on an object or feature.

abilities.label: Read
abilities.description: Read a document, screen, note, or log.

abilities.label: Ask IRIS
abilities.description: Ask IRIS questions in natural language about the case, the room, or discovered clues.

abilities.label: Compare
abilities.description: Ask IRIS to compare two pieces of evidence.

abilities.label: Accuse
abilities.description: Accuse a suspect and present a theory of the case.

globalImageStyle.description: Moody, semi-realistic digital illustration with cinematic lighting, slightly desaturated colors, strong atmospheric detail, consistent perspective, and subtle noir-futurist smart-home elements. Each room should feel inhabited but tense, with visual storytelling and some clues visible in the scene.

suspects.name: Lucas Thorne
suspects.publicDescription: Elias's grandson. Charming, financially unstable, and outwardly grief-stricken.
suspects.motive: Wanted the full life insurance payout for himself and did not want to split the money with Elias after the fake death.
suspects.opportunity: Arrived unexpectedly at the house while Elias was staging the fake death.
suspects.publicAlibi: Claims he arrived after the incident and found the aftermath.
suspects.hiddenTruth: Lucas killed Elias with the brass telescope in the study, then attempted to wipe the home's AI system and left the partial fake death staging in place.

suspects.name: Mara Quinn
suspects.publicDescription: Elias's live-in aide. Competent, guarded, and frustrated by Elias's secrecy.
suspects.motive: Possible resentment, professional burnout, and concern over Elias's erratic behavior.
suspects.opportunity: Was in the house earlier that evening.
suspects.publicAlibi: Left for town before the estimated time of death and was seen at a pharmacy.
suspects.hiddenTruth: Mara is innocent. She knew Elias was hiding something, but not the fake death plan.

suspects.name: Owen Briggs
suspects.publicDescription: The house groundskeeper. Practical, quiet, and often on the property late.
suspects.motive: Had argued with Elias over unpaid repair invoices.
suspects.opportunity: Was working outside in the rain near the terrace earlier in the evening.
suspects.publicAlibi: Left after finishing a repair on the cliffside railing.
suspects.hiddenTruth: Owen is innocent. His presence and muddy boots create a red herring.

rooms.name: Foyer
rooms.description: A narrow stone foyer with tall windows overlooking the black ocean. Rain streaks the glass. A damp wool coat hangs from a brass stand beside a silent security panel. The air smells faintly of salt, wet wool, and old electronics.
rooms.imageNotes: Show dramatic windows with crashing waves visible outside, a wet coat on a stand, a dim security panel, and a subtle sense of tension.

rooms.name: Main Hall
rooms.description: A long hall lined with portraits, dark wood, and discreet smart-home fixtures almost hidden in the trim. Muddy smears mar the polished floor near the terrace side, though they stop abruptly where they should continue.
rooms.imageNotes: Show a grand but slightly empty hallway, oil portraits, hidden smart-home touches, and muddy partial footprints that look suspiciously staged.

rooms.name: Study
rooms.description: Elias's study is crowded with old books, maritime charts, a heavy desk, and a brass telescope mounted near the window. A terminal on the desk glows faintly. One desk drawer sits slightly ajar. The room smells of paper, metal, and something recently cleaned.
rooms.imageNotes: Show the brass telescope, active computer terminal, open drawer, and a subtle hint of a struggle. This is one of the most important rooms.

rooms.name: Server Closet
rooms.description: A narrow hidden room behind a sliding shelf panel. Rack-mounted local servers hum softly. The air is warmer here. A maintenance tablet rests on a shelf beside a blinking local storage unit.
rooms.imageNotes: Show a secretive little tech room hidden behind the study, with racks, cables, a maintenance tablet, and a cool blue glow.

rooms.name: Kitchen
rooms.description: A high-end kitchen with stone counters, smart appliances, and a breakfast table left half-cleared. A tea tray sits near the sink. One mug has been rinsed. Another has not.
rooms.imageNotes: Show a stylish modern kitchen with one rinsed mug, one unrinsed mug, and a tea tray as a visual clue.

rooms.name: Pantry
rooms.description: A dry, orderly pantry with old preserves, smart inventory tags, and a locked lower cabinet. The room feels oddly over-organized.
rooms.imageNotes: Show shelves, labeled jars, discreet smart tags, and a locked cabinet that feels important.

rooms.name: Dining Room
rooms.description: A formal dining room lit by a low chandelier. One place setting remains partly disturbed. The terrace doors stand shut, but the curtains near them are torn as if someone wanted the room to look violated.
rooms.imageNotes: Show a formal room with disturbed place setting, torn curtain near terrace doors, and ominous lighting.

rooms.name: Conservatory
rooms.description: A glass-walled conservatory filled with salt-tolerant plants and antique seating. Moisture beads on the panes. A decorative walking cane leans near the door, and the room feels less disturbed than the house wants you to believe.
rooms.imageNotes: Show lush but restrained plant life, fogged glass, and a sense that this room was not truly part of a violent break-in.

rooms.name: Greenhouse
rooms.description: A humid greenhouse attached to the conservatory. Shelves hold seed trays, pruning tools, and storage bins. A plastic crate in the back contains supplies that do not belong in a greenhouse at all.
rooms.imageNotes: Show a more utilitarian plant room with bins and storage. A tucked-away crate should hint at hidden suspicious materials.

rooms.name: Stair Hall
rooms.description: A central stair hall with a sweeping staircase, soft wall lights, and a landing above. The smart lighting flickers once, then steadies.
rooms.imageNotes: Show elegant stairs and a slightly haunted high-tech atmosphere.

rooms.name: Upper Hall
rooms.description: A quiet upper corridor connecting the private rooms. The ocean can be heard through the walls more than seen.
rooms.imageNotes: Show a quiet upper hallway with closed bedroom doors and moody ambient lighting.

rooms.name: Master Bedroom
rooms.description: Elias's bedroom is austere and precise. A wardrobe stands open. On the nightstand sits a reading lamp and a framed photo of Elias with Lucas taken years ago, back when they still looked happy.
rooms.imageNotes: Show an older man's room, tasteful but sparse, with a sentimental photo that matters emotionally.

rooms.name: Guest Room
rooms.description: A neat guest room used occasionally by Mara. A medical tote rests on a chair. Everything is tidy enough to invite suspicion.
rooms.imageNotes: Show a neat room with a medical bag or tote that creates a red herring.

rooms.name: Lucas's Room
rooms.description: A stylish guest suite used by Lucas during visits. Expensive-looking clothes are draped carelessly over a chair. His travel duffel sits partly unzipped near the bed.
rooms.imageNotes: Show a polished room with signs of recent use, a duffel bag, and subtle hints of financial trouble and carelessness.

rooms.name: Cliffside Terrace
rooms.description: A stone terrace overlooking the crashing sea below. Wind lashes the furniture and the repaired railing gleams with fresh hardware. The space suggests a dramatic intrusion, but some details do not agree.
rooms.imageNotes: Show the open cliffside, wet stone, ocean below, repaired railing, and enough environmental drama to support the staged break-in theory.

objects.name: Wet Coat
objects.description: A dark wool coat, damp at the shoulders and cuffs.

examine.text: The coat is still damp. The rain ended earlier than the current death timeline suggests.

search.text: A crumpled dry-cleaning slip is tucked in one pocket.

objects.name: Dry-Cleaning Receipt
objects.description: A receipt timestamped that afternoon, showing Elias picked up the coat earlier the same day.

read.text: The coat was picked up clean and dry that afternoon. It was definitely exposed to rain later.

objects.name: Security Panel
objects.description: A wall-mounted smart home security panel currently showing partial system sync errors.

examine.text: The security panel cannot load a complete event list. It reports missing files and partial restore status.

objects.name: Muddy Footprints
objects.description: A line of muddy prints near the terrace side of the hall.

examine.text: The prints are strangely uniform in depth and stop too neatly. They look placed rather than naturally tracked.

objects.name: Hall Runner
objects.description: A long runner rug with a faint area of recent scrubbing.

search.text: A faint reddish-brown trace has been scrubbed from the rug fibers.

objects.name: Microfiber Cloth
objects.description: A damp microfiber cloth shoved under a side table.

examine.text: The cloth carries diluted blood traces and metal polish residue.

objects.name: Desk Terminal
objects.description: An active desktop terminal still logged into Elias's local account.

read.text: Open windows include an insurance policy document, a draft message, and a local AI admin console with failed deletion errors.

search.text: You find a partially saved document and a cache of recent local messages.

objects.name: Draft Split Note
objects.description: A partially written message from Elias to Lucas.

read.text: The note reads, in part: 'Once the claim clears, we wait. Then you transfer half through the Valencia account. No contact until then.'

objects.name: Insurance Folder
objects.description: A physical folder containing policy summaries and beneficiary paperwork.

read.text: Lucas Thorne is the sole beneficiary of a very large life insurance policy.

objects.name: Whiskey Glasses
objects.description: Two heavy tumblers on a side table, one with a fingerprint-smudged rim.

examine.text: Two people were recently in this room together. One glass bears fresh prints and lipstick-free residue inconsistent with Elias's habits.

objects.name: Brass Telescope
objects.description: A heavy brass telescope mounted near the window.

examine.text: The base has been recently wiped, but not perfectly.

objects.name: Cleaned Blood Trace
objects.description: A faint blood trace caught in a seam of metal and wood.

examine.text: This is a real injury trace, not part of the broader staged scene. The pattern suggests blunt force impact.

objects.name: Local Server Rack
objects.description: Local house servers storing security, environmental, and assistant logs.

examine.text: Several system services were manually deleted, but cache processes remained active during the attempt.

objects.name: Maintenance Tablet
objects.description: A service tablet still authenticated to the local admin tools.

use.text: You access residual event logs and recover a user ID associated with the deletion attempt.

objects.name: Partial Deletion Log
objects.description: A residual system record showing attempted deletion of house AI logs.

read.text: User token: LTHORNE-GUEST. Deletion attempt initiated at 20:55. Multiple files skipped because they were active or locked.

objects.name: Tea Tray
objects.description: A tray with a kettle, sugar, and two mugs.

search.text: One mug was rinsed in haste. Another still retains residue.

objects.name: Sedative Residue
objects.description: Trace sedative residue found in the unrinsed mug.

examine.text: The residue matches Elias's prescribed sleep aid. It could explain why he was easier to overpower, but it was self-administered or routine, not a murder method.

objects.name: Locked Lower Cabinet
objects.description: A locked cabinet tucked at floor level.

objects.name: Fake Blood Kit
objects.description: A small kit containing theatrical blood packets, adhesive wounds, and cleanup wipes.

examine.text: This was clearly part of a staged death setup, not a spontaneous home invasion.

objects.name: Terrace Doors
objects.description: Tall glass doors leading to the terrace.

examine.text: The lock shows no forced entry.

objects.name: Door Sensor Mismatch
objects.description: Sensor export indicating the terrace doors were never opened during the supposed intrusion window.

read.text: If an intruder came through the terrace, these logs should show it. They do not.

objects.name: Torn Curtain
objects.description: A curtain torn near the terrace door.

examine.text: The tear is theatrical and oddly clean, as if made for appearance rather than force.

objects.name: Storage Crate
objects.description: A plastic crate tucked behind seed trays.

search.text: Inside are odd supplies, including a molded boot sole and bits of costume wound makeup.

objects.name: Boot Stamp Tool
objects.description: A molded rubber sole attached to a handle, suitable for planting fake footprints.

examine.text: This explains the strangely uniform muddy prints in the hall.

objects.name: Shoeprint Cast
objects.description: A small cast impression left on a bench shelf.

examine.text: The cast matches the artificial boot stamp, not Owen's actual work boots.

objects.name: Second Phone
objects.description: A second phone hidden in a wardrobe pocket.

read.text: Recent messages confirm Elias arranged a later rendezvous after the payout and referred cryptically to 'our arrangement' with Lucas.

objects.name: Medical Tote
objects.description: Mara's organized medical tote.

search.text: Standard medications, records, and nothing that suggests foul play.

objects.name: Pharmacy Receipt
objects.description: A receipt showing Mara made a purchase in town during the estimated murder window.

objects.name: Travel Duffel
objects.description: Lucas's travel bag, partly unzipped.

search.text: You find a debt notice and a shirt cuff torn at the seam.

objects.name: Debt Notice
objects.description: An overdue collection notice showing significant financial pressure.

read.text: Lucas was in more trouble than he let on. The insurance payout would solve a great many problems.

objects.name: Torn Shirt Cuff
objects.description: A fine dress shirt cuff torn and faintly stained.

examine.text: The tear is consistent with a close struggle. The faint stain matches cleaned blood traces found elsewhere.

objects.name: Railing Section
objects.description: A cliffside railing with one freshly repaired segment.

examine.text: The repair is solid. There is no sign of a recent fall or impact here.

objects.name: Wet Stone Floor
objects.description: Rain-slick stone exposed to the sea wind.

search.text: There are no meaningful exit traces leading away from the house.

objects.name: No Exit Trace
objects.description: Environmental reading and visible inspection showing no credible path used by an intruder to enter or leave.

read.text: The scene wants to suggest an outside intruder, but the terrace does not support that theory.

objects.name: Study Key
objects.description: A small brass key tucked in the desk drawer.

examine.text: A brass key with a pantry tag.

objects.name: Desk Drawer
objects.description: A drawer left slightly open.

open.text: Inside are stationery, an old key, and a velvet cloth.

clues.title: Someone came in from the rain later than expected
clues.text: The wet coat suggests someone returned after the rain should have already ended.

clues.title: The coat was dry earlier that day
clues.text: The coat was picked up dry that afternoon, confirming it became wet later that evening.

clues.title: The house system was partially erased
clues.text: The security system reports missing files and partial restore status, indicating manual tampering.

clues.title: The muddy footprints look planted
clues.text: The footprints are too neat and too uniform to be genuine.

clues.title: Someone cleaned blood and metal residue
clues.text: A cleaning cloth carries diluted blood and metal polish residue.

clues.title: Important files were open during the deletion attempt
clues.text: Open windows and cache activity explain why some evidence survived the system wipe.

clues.title: Elias was planning a fake death with Lucas
clues.text: A draft note makes clear Elias expected Lucas to receive the insurance payout and secretly split it with him later.

clues.title: Lucas is the sole beneficiary
clues.text: The insurance policy names Lucas as sole beneficiary of a very large payout.

clues.title: Elias was not alone in the study
clues.text: Two whiskey glasses indicate Elias met with someone privately before the murder.

clues.title: The brass telescope was used in the real killing
clues.text: Blood trace and cleaning marks on the telescope base suggest blunt force trauma.

clues.title: The wipe failed because files were in use
clues.text: Server activity confirms some evidence survived because active files could not be deleted cleanly.

clues.title: Lucas initiated the deletion attempt
clues.text: A residual log shows a deletion attempt using Lucas's guest credentials.

clues.title: Elias had a sedative in his tea
clues.text: A sedative trace suggests Elias was somewhat impaired, but it appears to have been routine or self-administered rather than the murder method.

clues.title: A fake-death kit was hidden in the pantry
clues.text: The theatrical blood kit proves the original scene was intended to be staged.

clues.title: No one came through the terrace doors
clues.text: Door sensor logs contradict the idea of a terrace intruder.

clues.title: The break-in damage was theatrical
clues.text: The torn curtain appears performative rather than forceful.

clues.title: A tool was used to fake the footprints
clues.text: The boot stamp tool in the greenhouse explains the fake muddy prints.

clues.title: The groundskeeper's prints do not match
clues.text: The fake print tooling does not match Owen's actual work boots.

clues.title: Elias had a secret arrangement with Lucas
clues.text: Messages on Elias's hidden phone confirm an off-books plan between him and Lucas.

clues.title: Mara has an independent alibi
clues.text: The pharmacy receipt supports Mara's claim that she was away during the murder window.

clues.title: Lucas was under severe financial pressure
clues.text: Debt notices show Lucas had urgent money problems.

clues.title: Lucas was involved in a close struggle
clues.text: The torn cuff and staining suggest Lucas was physically involved in the fatal confrontation.

clues.title: There was no fatal fall from the terrace
clues.text: The railing repair is intact and there is no sign of impact or collapse.

clues.title: There is no credible intruder escape route
clues.text: The terrace surface and conditions do not support entry or exit by an outside attacker.

clues.title: A key links the study to the hidden staging materials
clues.text: The pantry cabinet key was kept in Elias's study, tying him directly to the hidden fake-death kit.

timeline.event: Mara serves Elias tea and leaves him in stable condition.
Owen finishes terrace railing work and departs the exterior grounds.
Rain tapers off along the cliffside.
Elias opens the insurance files and local AI admin console in the study.
Elias begins planting fake evidence for a staged violent death.
Elias drafts a message to Lucas explaining how to split the payout after the fake death is accepted.
Lucas arrives unexpectedly at the house.
Lucas and Elias drink whiskey in the study and argue over the payout arrangement.
Lucas kills Elias with the brass telescope during the argument.
Lucas uses guest credentials to wipe the house AI logs and leaves the fake break-in evidence in place.
The staged scene is left behind, but active files, caches, and residual logs survive.

redHerrings.description: Mara's medical role makes her seem suspicious at first, especially with Elias having sedative residue in his tea.
Owen's outdoor work and muddy boots make the staged footprint evidence initially point toward him.
The torn curtain, muddy prints, and terrace setting are designed to suggest an outside intruder.

solution.method: Blunt force trauma using the brass telescope in the study
solution.motive: Lucas discovered that Elias intended to split the insurance payout after faking his death. Already under serious debt pressure, Lucas killed Elias so he could keep the full amount.
solution.coverStory: Lucas left Elias's partially staged fake break-in scene in place, then tried to wipe the house AI logs so it would look like a violent intrusion or suspicious accident.
solution.fullExplanation: Elias Thorne never intended to die that night. He was staging a false violent death so his grandson Lucas could collect a large life insurance payout and later share it with him in secret. Elias had already hidden fake blood materials, planted false break-in signs, and begun drafting instructions to Lucas. When Lucas arrived and realized Elias expected half the insurance money after the claim paid out, he confronted him in the study. The argument escalated. Lucas struck Elias with the brass telescope, killing him for real. He then tried to erase the house AI records using guest credentials. The wipe was incomplete because several study files and cache processes were still active. Those surviving fragments, along with the hidden staging materials and Lucas's financial motive, reveal the truth.

endingText.success: IRIS goes quiet for a moment after your accusation. Then, softly: 'The house was trying to remember a lie and a murder at the same time. You separated them. Elias built a fraud. Lucas turned it into a killing.' Outside, the waves strike the cliff again and again, as if the house itself is finally done holding its breath.
endingText.failure: IRIS's reply is measured. 'That theory does not survive the evidence we have recovered. The house is still remembering. We are not finished.'

engineHints.visibilityRule: Hidden objects and hidden clues should not be surfaced by IRIS unless discovered through valid play.
engineHints.narrationRule: AI narration may embellish mood and description but may not invent new facts, objects, exits, or evidence not present in this JSON.
engineHints.stateRule: The engine should store room visits, discovered clues, inventory, unlocked containers, and accusation state outside the scenario definition.
